mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal broad economic strength, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positively impacting equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: A positive 3-month change in PAYEMS is followed by negative S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3%, it triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank, increasing the discount rate for future earnings and causing negative equity returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Standard central bank inflation targeting mandates
failure_condition: In periods where YoY CPIAUCSL > 3.0%, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising retail sales (RSAFS) and rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based, sustainable consumer demand that supports corporate revenue growth. The signal is only active when both indicators are positive, as strong retail sales without housing support may be unsustainable.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS > 0 AND 3-month change in HOUST > 0
failure_condition: The strategy of buying SPX when both proxies show positive 3-month momentum underperforms a buy-and-hold strategy over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion with low unemployment, a further drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals an overheating labor market, leading to wage-push inflation fears and pre-emptive monetary tightening, which negatively impacts equity markets.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 4.0%, a further 3-month decline is not followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns on average.

mechanism_narrative: Positive surprises or upward momentum in Industrial Production (INDPRO) reflect a robust manufacturing and supply side of the economy, leading to immediate positive revisions in earnings forecasts and short-term positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 1-month change in INDPRO and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary policy tightening (rising FEDFUNDS) concurrent with a weakening labor market (rising UNRATE) creates a high-risk environment for recession. This 'hiking into weakness' interaction sharply increases risk premia, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in FEDFUNDS > 0 AND 3-month change in UNRATE > 0
failure_condition: Periods where both FEDFUNDS and UNRATE are rising are not followed by statistically significant negative 60-day S&P 500 returns compared to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real economic activity, proxied by the quarter-over-quarter annualized growth of Real GDP (GDPC1) falling below 1%, signals an imminent recession, causing a flight to safety and negative returns for equities.
failure_condition: Following a GDPC1 growth print below 1%, the S&P 500 posts positive returns over the next 60 days in more than 50% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is a primary, non-revisable indicator of economic deterioration, leading to reduced consumer spending, lower corporate profits, and negative equity market performance.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is a key NBER recession indicator
failure_condition: The rolling 36-month correlation between the 3-month change in UNRATE and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive.
